homepage.heroHeadline: Trusted Home Experts in Salt Lake City
homepage.heroSubline: Apex General Contractor brings reliability to every project. Serving Sandy, Draper, Lehi, and Provo with quality craftsmanship tailored to Utah's climate.
aboutText: Founded in 2012, Apex General Contractor started as a small family-owned business serving historic homes in Salt Lake City's Avenues neighborhood. Over a decade later, we've grown to meet the demands of Utah's expanding communities while maintaining our personal touch.

We approach every project with meticulous attention to detail, especially important given Utah's freeze-thaw cycles and seismic activity. Our team specializes in solutions that stand up to mountain weather while preserving your home's character.
As active members of the Salt Lake Valley Builders Association, we're committed to sustainable practices that protect our beautiful Wasatch Front environment while strengthening local homes.

valueProps.title: Climate-Smart Building
valueProps.description: We specialize in materials and techniques that withstand Utah's heavy snow loads, temperature swings, and dry air.

valueProps.title: Neighborhood Specialists
valueProps.description: From Sugar House bungalows to Draper's modern developments, we understand each area's unique architectural needs.

valueProps.title: Transparent Process
valueProps.description: Daily updates and clear pricing mean no surprises - just quality work you can trust.

valueProps.title: Local Partnerships
valueProps.description: We source materials from Utah suppliers whenever possible, supporting our community's economy.

homepage.ctaHeadline: Ready to Build Confidence in Your Home?
homepage.ctaSubline: Schedule a free consultation to discuss your project with our Salt Lake City team.

services.name: Roof Replacement
services.shortDescription: Durable roofing solutions designed for Utah's heavy snow seasons.
services.description:
Living along the Wasatch Front means preparing for winter's heavy snow loads. We replace aging roofs with materials rated for mountain conditions, paying special attention to ice dam prevention and proper attic ventilation.

Our process begins with a thorough inspection of your existing roof structure and insulation needs. For historic homes in areas like The Avenues, we carefully match original materials while upgrading underlying support. In newer developments like Draper, we install impact-resistant shingles that handle temperature swings.

Every installation includes snow load calculations specific to your neighborhood's elevation and typical snowfall. We also address Utah's dry air concerns with proper moisture barriers to prevent wood shrinkage and cracking over time.
process: Step 1: Free inspection assessing snow load capacity and ventilation needs
Step 2: Customized material selection for your home's style and climate demands
Step 3: Careful tear-off with full debris containment
Step 4: Precision installation with extended weatherproofing warranties
benefits: Snow load-rated materials prevent winter damage

faq.question: How often should Utah roofs be replaced?
faq.answer: Most roofs last 15-25 years here depending on materials. South-facing slopes degrade faster due to intense sun exposure before winter storms.

faq.question: Do you work during winter months?
faq.answer: Yes, with proper precautions. We schedule tear-offs on clear days and use temporary covers when needed to protect your home.

faq.question: Can you match my historic home's original roofing?
faq.answer: We maintain relationships with specialty suppliers to source appropriate materials for Salt Lake's older neighborhoods whenever possible.

services.name: Foundation Repair
services.shortDescription: Expert solutions for Utah's freeze-thaw and seismic challenges.
services.description:
Utah's dramatic temperature changes and seismic activity create unique foundation stresses. We specialize in diagnosing and repairing settlement cracks, heaving concrete, and earthquake-related shifts common throughout Salt Lake Valley.

Our team begins with laser-level measurements to identify movement patterns. For older homes in areas like Sugar House, we often find original footings inadequate for modern use. In rapidly growing areas like Lehi's tech corridor, we address soil compaction issues from recent construction.

Repairs may include helical piers for lift correction, epoxy injections for hairline cracks, or complete underpinning systems. Every solution considers future freeze-thaw cycles and includes drainage improvements to prevent recurring issues.
process: Step 1: Comprehensive evaluation using digital leveling tools
Step 2: Custom repair plan addressing root causes
Step 3: Minimally invasive techniques when possible
Step 4: Final inspection with long-term monitoring recommendations

faq.question: How can I tell if my foundation needs repair?
faq.answer: Look for diagonal cracks wider than 1/4", sticking doors, or gaps between walls and ceilings. These often appear after our harsh winters.

faq.question: Does homeowners insurance cover foundation repairs?
faq.answer: Sometimes - we'll help document damage sources to support your claim, especially for seismic or plumbing-related issues.

faq.question: Will repairs disrupt my landscaping?
faq.answer: We use directional drilling when possible to minimize surface disturbance, especially important in established neighborhoods.

services.name: Whole House Remodel
services.shortDescription: Transform your space while respecting Utah home character.
services.description:
Whether updating a 1940s bungalow in Capitol Hill or modernizing a 1990s Sandy family home, we approach whole-house remodels with sensitivity to original architecture and modern living needs.

Our design-build process begins with understanding how your household uses space. We then develop plans that improve flow while maintaining structural integrity - especially important in Utah's earthquake zone. For historic districts, we navigate preservation guidelines while incorporating contemporary comforts.

Common projects include opening main floor layouts, adding mountain-view windows, or creating multi-generational suites. We pay special attention to energy efficiency upgrades that combat temperature extremes, like spray foam insulation and high-performance windows.
process: Step 1: Lifestyle assessment and goal setting
Step 2: Architectural planning with 3D renderings
Step 3: Phased construction minimizing disruption
Step 4: Final walkthrough with maintenance guidance

faq.question: How long do whole-house remodels typically take?
faq.answer: Most projects take 4-8 months depending on scope. We create detailed timelines accounting for material lead times and weather delays.

faq.question: Can you help with historic district approvals?
faq.answer: Yes, we're experienced working with Salt Lake's Landmark Commission and can guide you through the process.

faq.question: Do you handle kitchen/bath updates during remodels?
faq.answer: Absolutely - we coordinate all trades to ensure seamless integration of these complex spaces.

services.name: Deck Construction
services.shortDescription: Year-round outdoor living spaces built for mountain weather.
services.description:
Utah's dramatic seasons demand decks that withstand blazing summer sun and heavy winter snow. We design and build durable outdoor spaces using materials proven in our climate, from composite decking that resists drying to reinforced footings that prevent frost heave.

For homes along the benches with panoramic views, we incorporate glass railings to preserve sightlines. In shaded areas like Liberty Park neighborhoods, we select slip-resistant surfaces that handle morning dew and afternoon shade. Every design considers proper drainage to prevent ice buildup on stairs and walkways.

We're particularly experienced with hillside installations common in Draper and Sandy, where proper grading and retaining walls ensure long-term stability. All structures meet current snow load requirements and include hidden fasteners to prevent surface checking.
process: Step 1: Site evaluation assessing sun exposure and drainage
Step 2: Material selection meeting your aesthetic and maintenance preferences
Step 3: Precision framing with hidden structural reinforcements
Step 4: Final inspection with care instructions
benefits: Materials chosen for Utah's UV exposure
Engineered for safety during freeze-thaw cycles

faq.question: Should I enclose my deck for winter use?
faq.answer: We can discuss three-season room options that handle snow loads while extending your living space.

faq.question: How do you prevent wood decking from drying out?
faq.answer: We use premium cedar or composite materials with UV inhibitors, plus recommend annual sealing for natural wood.

faq.question: Can you match my home's existing trim details?
faq.answer: Yes, we'll replicate architectural elements like railings and posts to maintain cohesive design.

services.name: Bathroom Remodel
services.shortDescription: Luxurious yet practical baths for Utah's dry climate.
services.description:
Utah's low humidity affects everything from grout longevity to wood vanity care. Our bathroom remodels address these unique challenges while creating spa-like retreats suited to your home's era and your family's needs.

For historic homes in areas like 9th and 9th, we preserve original layouts while upgrading plumbing and ventilation. In student-heavy Provo neighborhoods near BYU, we design durable shared baths with easy-clean surfaces. All projects include proper vapor barriers and humidity-controlled exhaust systems to prevent drywall damage.

We specialize in water-saving fixtures that perform well with our mineral-rich water, from pressure-balanced shower valves to easy-drain tile patterns. For primary suites, we often incorporate heated floors - a welcome luxury during cold Wasatch Front winters.
process: Step 1: Consultation assessing your water usage and storage needs
Step 2: Design phase selecting moisture-resistant materials
Step 3: Careful demolition protecting existing structures
Step 4: Installation with daily cleanup
benefits: Materials selected for hard water resistance

faq.question: How do you prevent grout discoloration from our water?
faq.answer: We use premium epoxy grout and recommend whole-house water softeners for areas with particularly hard water.

faq.question: Can you work within my existing plumbing layout?
faq.answer: In most cases yes, though we may suggest relocating fixtures for better functionality given space constraints.

faq.question: What's the best flooring for Utah bathrooms?
faq.answer: Porcelain tile with textured finish provides warmth and slip resistance - we avoid materials that shrink like certain engineered woods.

services.name: Kitchen Remodel
services.description:
Utah kitchens need to handle everything from holiday feasts to everyday meal prep while standing up to our unique climate. We design culinary spaces that balance beauty and practicality, using materials that resist drying and temperature fluctuations.

For older homes in Capitol Hill, we preserve original cabinet frames while upgrading interiors with modern storage solutions. In fast-growing areas like Lehi, we create tech-friendly kitchens with charging stations and smart lighting. All designs include proper ventilation to combat winter inversion cooking smells.

We pay special attention to countertop selections - avoiding materials prone to cracking in dry air - and cabinet finishes that won't check over time. Pantry designs often incorporate extra storage for bulk goods common in Utah households.
process: Step 1: Cooking style assessment and storage audit
Step 2: Layout planning with traffic flow considerations
Step 3: Careful demolition protecting adjacent spaces
Step 4: Installation with daily cleanup
benefits: Materials selected for durability in dry climates

faq.question: How do you handle permits for kitchen remodels?
faq.answer: We secure all required Salt Lake Valley permits and coordinate inspections, especially important for electrical and gas line updates.

faq.question: What countertops hold up best in Utah?
faq.answer: Quartz resists drying better than natural stone, while porcelain slab handles temperature swings beautifully.

faq.question: Can you match my historic home's original cabinets?
faq.answer: Our finish specialists can replicate most vintage stains and hardware profiles when refinishing is preferred over replacement.

services.name: Window Replacement
services.shortDescription: Energy-efficient windows for Utah's temperature extremes.
services.description:
Salt Lake Valley's hot summers and cold winters demand windows that insulate effectively while standing up to freeze-thaw cycles. We replace aging single-pane windows with high-performance units that reduce energy bills and outside noise.

For historic districts like The Avenues, we offer authentic divided-light designs that meet preservation guidelines. In modern developments like Daybreak, we install sleek low-E glass that combats UV exposure. All installations include proper flashing and insulation to prevent air leaks common in our dry climate.

We particularly focus on south-facing windows that bear the brunt of summer heat and winter storms. Our team can advise on optimal glazing choices for each exposure and help qualify for Utah's energy efficiency rebates.
process: Step 1: Energy audit identifying priority windows
Step 2: Product selection balancing performance and aesthetics
Step 3: Careful removal protecting interior finishes
Step 4: Precision installation with weatherproof sealing

faq.question: How much can new windows save on energy bills?
faq.answer: Most Utah homeowners see 15-25% reductions, especially noticeable during January inversions and July heat waves.

faq.question: Do you replace windows year-round?
faq.answer: Yes, with proper precautions. We use temporary interior seals when working during extreme temperatures.

faq.question: Can you match my original wood window trim?
faq.answer: Our carpenters meticulously replicate historic profiles using durable modern materials when originals can't be preserved.

reviews.name: Meredith K.
reviews.text: After last winter's heavy snow caused leaks in our Sugar House bungalow, Apex installed a beautiful standing-seam metal roof that handles snow slides perfectly. They preserved our original dormer details while adding proper ice/water shield. The crew was meticulous about cleaning up nails and old shingles every day.

reviews.name: James T.
reviews.text: Our Draper home developed concerning cracks after the 2024 earthquakes. Apex's foundation specialists installed helical piers that lifted our sunken porch perfectly. They even repaired our landscaping better than before. Most importantly, they explained everything in terms we could understand.

reviews.name: Sophia R.
reviews.text: Apex transformed our 1950s Avenues bathroom from cramped to luxurious without losing its vintage charm. They suggested penny tile that hides mineral deposits from our hard water and installed a quiet exhaust fan that actually vents outside (unlike our old one). The heated floor gets daily use November through March!

reviews.name: Carlos M.
reviews.text: Our new composite deck in Sandy handles snow removal like a champ and doesn't get scorching hot in summer. The crew built custom planters that match our home's trim and designed railings that keep our toddlers safe without blocking the mountain view. Two winters in and zero issues with heaving or cracking.

reviews.name: Lisa W.
reviews.text: As a serious home cook, I appreciated how Apex designed our Provo kitchen remodel around workflow. The quartz countertops stay cool for pastry work, and the new range hood actually eliminates cooking smells during inversion season. They even incorporated my grandmother's antique pie safe into the new design seamlessly.

reviews.name: Derek H.
reviews.text: Replacing our 1980s aluminum windows with energy-efficient ones made an immediate difference in our Liberty Park home's comfort. No more drafts during winter storms! Apex matched the original divided-light look perfectly and even repaired some rotted sills we hadn't noticed. Our heating bill dropped 20% this January.

service-areas.title: Serving Sandy, Draper, Lehi & Provo Homes
service-areas.description: Local general-contractor proudly serving Utah County and Salt Lake County communities with neighborhood-specific construction solutions.

serviceAreas.content: Apex General Contractor proudly serves Sandy, UT, providing top-tier construction and remodeling services for homes primarily built in the 1980s-90s. Our expertise in handling snowy winters and inversions ensures your property remains durable and energy-efficient year-round. Whether it's roof repairs to withstand heavy snowfall or basement waterproofing to combat moisture, we tailor solutions for Sandy's unique climate challenges. We also specialize in updating older homes with modern amenities while preserving their classic charm, making them more functional for today's families.
serviceAreas.localContext: Sandy features a mix of well-established neighborhoods with a strong community feel, perfect for families and long-term residents.

serviceAreas.content: In Draper, UT, Apex General Contractor specializes in working with newer developments from the 1990s to 2020s. Our team understands the specific needs of modern construction, ensuring seamless remodels, additions, and repairs. Draper's cold, snowy winters and inversions require homes to have proper insulation and weatherproofing—services we excel at providing. From custom home builds to updating contemporary designs, we help homeowners create spaces that are both stylish and resilient against Utah's harsh winters.
serviceAreas.localContext: Draper is known for its rapid growth, blending suburban comfort with access to tech hubs and outdoor recreation.

serviceAreas.content: Lehi, UT, is a booming tech corridor with rapid housing growth, and Apex General Contractor is here to meet the demands of this dynamic market. We specialize in new construction and remodeling projects tailored to modern families and professionals. Lehi's cold, snowy winters require homes built with durability in mind, and our expertise ensures your property is prepared for the elements. Whether you're building from scratch or upgrading an existing space, we deliver high-quality craftsmanship that aligns with Lehi's fast-paced lifestyle.
serviceAreas.localContext: Lehi combines tech-driven economic growth with family-friendly neighborhoods, making it a hotspot for young professionals.

serviceAreas.content: Serving Provo, UT, Apex General Contractor caters to a diverse housing market near Brigham Young University (BYU). From historic homes to student housing and modern builds, we handle renovations and repairs with precision. Provo's cold winters and inversions demand robust construction solutions, and we excel at improving energy efficiency and weather resistance. Whether you're a homeowner, landlord, or investor, we provide reliable services to enhance Provo's unique mix of properties.
serviceAreas.localContext: Provo offers a blend of academic vibrancy and tight-knit communities, with housing ranging from student rentals to upscale family homes.

serviceAreas.content: Apex General Contractor delivers exceptional construction and remodeling services in Midvale, UT, where mid-century homes meet modern updates. Our team is skilled in addressing the challenges of cold, snowy winters and inversions, ensuring your home stays comfortable and efficient. From kitchen remodels to full-home renovations, we help Midvale residents upgrade their living spaces while maintaining the area's classic appeal. Trust us to bring quality craftsmanship to your next project.
serviceAreas.localContext: Midvale is a centrally located city with a mix of historic charm and convenient access to Salt Lake Valley amenities.

serviceAreas.content: In South Jordan, UT, Apex General Contractor provides expert construction services for a growing community with diverse housing needs. Whether it's new builds in master-planned developments or updates to established homes, we prioritize durability against snowy winters and inversions. Our team specializes in creating functional, stylish spaces that cater to South Jordan's family-oriented lifestyle. From outdoor living areas to energy-efficient upgrades, we’re here to bring your vision to life.
serviceAreas.localContext: South Jordan is known for its high-quality schools, parks, and a balance of suburban and urban conveniences.

serviceAreas.content: Apex General Contractor serves Orem, UT, with top-notch remodeling and construction services tailored to its university-influenced housing market. From student rentals to family homes, we address the challenges of cold winters and inversions with weather-resistant solutions. Our projects range from basement finishes to exterior upgrades, all designed to enhance Orem's vibrant community. Let us help you create a space that meets your needs and withstands Utah's climate.
serviceAreas.localContext: Orem is a hub for education and family living, with a strong sense of community and diverse housing options.

serviceAreas.content: West Jordan, UT, benefits from Apex General Contractor's expertise in handling a wide range of construction and remodeling projects. Our team is well-versed in preparing homes for cold, snowy winters and inversions, offering solutions like improved insulation and storm-resistant roofing. Whether you're updating an older home or building new, we deliver reliable craftsmanship that fits West Jordan's evolving landscape. Trust us to enhance your property’s functionality and curb appeal.
serviceAreas.localContext: West Jordan offers a mix of suburban tranquility and urban amenities, with a growing population and diverse housing styles.
